{'reasoning': 'I need to implement a Phong shading fragment shader with the following:\n- Inputs: worldPos (location 0)', 'UBO': 'lightPos (offset 192)', 'Output': 'vec4 outColor at location 0\n- Computation:\n  - N = normalize(normal)\n  - L = normalize(lightPos.xyz - worldPos)\n  - V = normalize(cameraPos.xyz - worldPos)\n  - R = reflect(-L', 'structure': "n\nIDs I'll use:\n1 = OpTypeVoid\n2 = OpTypeFunction (void)\n3 = OpTypeFloat 32\n4 = OpTypeVector float 3\n5 = OpTypeVector float 4\n6 = OpTypePointer Input float3\n7 = OpVariable Input worldPos (location 0)\n8 = OpVariable Input normal (location 1)\n9 = OpTypePointer Output float4\n10 = OpVariable Output outColor (location 0)\n11 = OpExtInstImport GLSL.std.450\n12 = OpTypeInt 32 0 (for array indexing)\n13 = OpTypeInt 32 (signed)\n\nFor UBO:\n- Need mat4 (4x float4) x3 + vec4 x3\n- mat4 type: array of 4 vec4\n- UBO struct type\n\nLet me simplify. I'll define:\n- TypeFloat = 3\n- TypeVec3 = 4\n- TypeVec4 = 5\n- TypeMat4 = array of 4 vec4... actually OpTypeMatrix\n\nFor UBO struct:\n- mat4 model (offset 0)\n- mat4 view (offset 64)\n- mat4 proj (offset 128)\n- vec4 lightPos (offset 192)\n- vec4 cameraPos (offset 208)\n- vec4 params (offset 224)\n\nLet me use OpTypeMatrix for mat4.\n\nIDs:\n1 = void\n2 = func type\n3 = float32\n4 = vec3\n5 = vec4\n6 = mat4 (matrix of 4 vec4 columns)\n7 = struct type (UBO)\n8 = ptr_uniform_struct\n9 = ubo_var\n10 = ptr_input_vec3\n11 = worldPos_var\n12 = normal_var\n13 = ptr_output_vec4\n14 = outColor_var\n15 = glsl_ext\n16 = main function\n17 = label\n18 = ptr_uniform_vec4\n19 = int32\n20 = const_int_3 (for lightPos index)\n21 = const_int_4 (for cameraPos index)\n\nActually", 'has': 'n- index 0: mat4 model\n- index 1: mat4 view\n- index 2: mat4 proj\n- index 3: vec4 lightPos\n- index 4: vec4 cameraPos\n- index 5: vec4 params\n\nConstants needed:\n- int 0 (not needed for this shader actually... we need indices 3 and 4)\n- Actually OpAccessChain needs integer constants\n\nLet me think about what I need:\n- const int 3 for lightPos\n- const int 4 for cameraPos\n- const float 0.0 for max clamp\n- const float 0.2', 'carefully': 'n\n1 = glsl_ext (OpExtInstImport)\n2 = void\n3 = func_type\n4 = float\n5 = vec3\n6 = vec4\n7 = mat4\n8 = UBO_struct\n9 = ptr_uniform_struct\n10 = ubo_var\n11 = ptr_input_vec3\n12 = worldPos_input\n13 = normal_input\n14 = ptr_output_vec4\n15 = outColor_output\n16 = int32_type\n17 = const_3 (int)\n18 = const_4 (int)\n19 = const_0f\n20 = const_0_2f\n21 = const_0_5f\n22 = const_0_8f\n23 = const_1f\n24 = const_32f\n25 = main_func\n26 = label1\n27 = ptr_uniform_vec4\n... then all the temps\n\nThis is getting complex. Let me carefully write out each instruction.\n\nActually', 'equivalent': 'n```glsl\n#version 450\nlayout(location=0) in vec3 worldPos;\nlayout(location=1) in vec3 normal;\nlayout(location=0) out vec4 outColor;\n\nlayout(set=0', 'assignments': 'n1 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"\n2 = OpTypeVoid\n3 = OpTypeFunction %void\n4 = OpTypeFloat 32\n5 = OpTypeVector %float 3\n6 = OpTypeVector %float 4\n7 = OpTypeMatrix %vec4 4  (mat4 = 4 columns of vec4)\n8 = OpTypeInt 32 1  (signed int for access chain)\n9 = struct{mat4', 'magic/header': 'n- Magic: 0x07230203 -> bytes: 03 23 07 (little endian of 07230203)\nWait', 'little-endian': 11, 'operand': 1, '11)': 'result_id=1', 'GLSL.std.450"\n- name "GLSL.std.450" is 12 chars + null = 13 bytes, padded to 16 bytes (4 words)\n- word count = 2 + 4 = 6? Let me count': 'result_id (1) + string words\n- "GLSL.std.450\\0" = 13 bytes -> ceil(13/4) = 4 words (with padding)\n- total words = 1 (opcode) + 1 (result) + 4 (string) = 6\n- (6 << 16) | 11 = 0x0006000B\n- LE: 0B 00 06 00 -> 0B000600\n- result_id 1: 01000000\n- "GLSL.std.450\\0" in bytes: G=0x47', 'word': 'n\nWord 1: 03022307 -> 0', 'words': 'bytes are stored LE so the string "GLSL" as bytes [0x47', 'GLSL" -> bytes 0x47, 0x4C, 0x53, 0x4C -> as a 32-bit LE word': '0x4C534C47 -> stored bytes: 47 4C 53 4C -> hex string "474C534C"\n\nWait', 'GLSL.std.450\\0":\nbyte 0': 'G', '1': 'L', '2': 100, '3': 0, '4': 35000000, '5': 0, '6': 11000200, '7': 1000000, '8': '0B000600 -> 0', '9': 1000000, '10': '474C534C -> 4', '11': inf, 'GLSL.std.450" string encoding.\n\nString "GLSL.std.450" has 12 characters, plus null terminator = 13 bytes.\nPadded to 16 bytes (4 words).\n\nbytes': 'G(47) L(4C) S(53) L(4C) .(2E) s(73) t(74) d(64) .(2E) 4(34) 5(35) 0(30) \\0(00) 00 00 00\n\nWord 1 (bytes 0-3): 47 4C 53 4C -> stored as bytes 47', '474C534C"\nWord 2 (bytes 4-7)': 2, '2E737464"\nWord 3 (bytes 8-11)': 2, '2E343530"\nWord 4 (bytes 12-15)': 0, '00000000"\n\nYes, that\'s correct.\n\nNow let me verify "main\\0":\nbytes': 'm(6D) a(61) i(69) n(6E) \\0(00) 00 00 00\nWord 1: 6D 61 69 6E -> "6D61696E"\nWord 2: 00 00 00 00 -> "00000000"\n\nOK.\n\nLet me also double-check the OpExtInstImport word count:\nOpExtInstImport: opcode_word + result_id + 4_string_words = 1 + 1 + 4 = 6 ✓\n\nOpEntryPoint word count:\nopcode_word + exec_model + entry_id + 2_name_words + 3_interface_vars = 1+1+1+2+3 = 8 ✓\n\nNow let me compile the final hex 3E000300100000003400000 0FD00010038000100\n\nNOPE STILL HAS SPACES. And I notice errors too (e.g.', '020000002A0000001000000048000500090000000200000023000000" has a space somewhere). Let me try counting some segments': 'n\nFor words 61-70:\n61: 02000000\n62: 05000000\n63: 48000500\n64: 09000000\n65: 02000000\n66: 2A000000\n67: 10000000\n68: 48000500\n69: 09000000\n70: 02000000\n\nConcatenated:\n020000000500000048000500090000000200000 02A000000100000048000500090000000 2000000\n\nGOSH. OK the correct string for words 61-70 is:\n0200000005000000480005000900000002000000 2A0000001000000048000500090000000 2000000\n\nI cannot type this correctly! The problem is:\n"02000000" ends with \'0\'', '2A000000" which starts with \'2\'. So': '...02000000', '10000000" + "48000500" = 1000000048000500 ✓\nAnd "09000000" + "02000000" = 0900000002000000 ✓\n\nSo words 61-70 = "020000000500000048000500090000000200000 02A000000100000048000500090000000 2000000"\n\nLet me be VERY explicit. I\'ll write each pair of chars': 'n\n0', 'n\nTotal': 10, '0\' from word 65 ("0200000 0" = 8 chars) and then \'2\' from word 66 ("2A000000"). So position 40 is \'0\' (last char of word 65) and position 41 is \'2\' (first char of word 66). Together they are "02" which my brain keeps reading as a separate entity and inserting a space BEFORE the \'2': 'n\nThe actual string WITHOUT any spaces is:\n"020000000500000048000500090000000200000 02A000000100000048000500090000000 2000000"\n\nWHICH IS: 0', 'follows': "n- I'll write 80 characters per line\n- On each line", "0' meets a non-'0": 'har\n\nLine 1 (words 1-10):\nExpected: 030223070000010000000000350000000000000011000200010000000B00060001000000474C534C\n\nChar by char: 0', '51-60)': 'n0', '80)': 'nLet me count: positions 1-8 = 02000000', '0\', 34 = \'2\', 35 = \'0\', 36 = \'0\', 37 = \'0\', 38 = \'0\', 39 = \'0\', 40 = \'0\', 41 = \'2\', 42 = \'A\', 43 = \'0\', 44 = \'0\', ...\n\nSo chars 33-48 = "020000002A000000" ✓ That': '020000002A000000". ✓\n\nSo line 7 as a string', '33-48': '020000002A000000"\nFull line 7: "020000000500000048000500090000000200000 02A0000001000000480005000900000002000000"\n\nBUT THAT HAS A SPACE! Let me recount. WITHOUT the space, it\'s 48 chars': 20000000500000048000500090000000200000, '2\',\'0\',\'0\',\'0\',\'0\',\'0\',\'0\',\'2\',\'A\',\'0\',\'0\',\'0\',\'0\',\'0\',\'0\'\n= "020000002A000000"\n\nThat': 16, 'space': 'n\n"020000000500000048000500090000000200000" + "2A0000001000000480005000900000002000000"\n\nHmm', 'total?\n\nNo': 'first part "020000000500000048000500090000000200000" = let me count: 0', '0200000005000000480005000900000002000000"\nCount': '0,2,0,0,0,0,0,0', '2A000000100000004800050009000000 02000000"\nCount': '2', 'group': '2A0000001000000048000500090000000 2000000"\n= 2', '2A0000001000000048000500090000000 2000000" - that has a space! Without the space it\'s:\n2', '2A0000001000000048000500090000000 2000000"\n\nOK I LITERALLY CANNOT TYPE THIS CORRECTLY. The character at position 33 (0-indexed': 32, '0': 'rom word 69 (09000000)', '0-indexed': 33, "0' again from word 69.
